After publishing the business object, you must add the tile, which you created by checking the “User Interface” checkbox, to your business catalog. To find out how to do that, see How to Add Custom Catalog Extensions | SAP Help Portal. 

You can now open the tile from your Fiori Launchpad and add values for the fields you added. 

After that, you can now use this lookup table in the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Service Documents app. 

In the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Service Documents app, you create the substitution rule in the following way: 

Use the Precondition to specify the source field to be used for substitution. For this case, we are using the Sold-to Party. Make sure you set the Operator to “Is not empty”. 

After that, you specify the value and the target field to be used in the substitution. To do so, set the Target Field to Profit Center and use the Substitution Type “Table Lookup”. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

To find the correct value to be substituted, you have to specify the data source and the condition, which you can do using the Source field. To do so, open the value help of the Source field and specify a Data Source and the Source Field. As the Data Source, select the lookup table you created earlier in the Custom Business Objects app. As the Source Field, select the Identifier of the Profit Center field that you used in the lookup table. 

You can now set the conditions that specify when the field will be substituted. To do so, in the Conditions field, select the Identifier of the Sold-to Party you used in the lookup table. As the Operator, you have to use “Equal to FieldFunction”. In the Value field, you then have to select the Identifier of the Sold-to Party (SoldToParty) field in the Service Document. 

You can check the “Overwrite” checkbox to make sure the value you determined for the profit center overwrites any previously filled-in value in the target field. 

After you have activated the rule, the system checks whether the Sold-to Party field filled in the Service Document equals the Sold-to Party field in the lookup table. If this is true, the corresponding profit center from the lookup table will be used in the Service Contract.  

This should save you some time during profit center determination, and if you want to look further, here are some resources from SAP Help: 

  • Profit Center Determination for Service Transactions 
  • Manage Substitution/Validation Rules - Service Documents 
  • Creating Custom Business Objects | SAP Help Portal 
  • Creating Extension Items and Transporting Them | SAP Help Portal 
  • Create Custom Business Objects in S/4HANA Cloud | SAP Tutorials

entry.title: Two-Tier ERP with SAP Cloud ERP - 2602 Release Highlights
entry.content:
I am pleased to share the new addition from Two-Tier ERP as part of the 2602 release for  SAP Cloud ERP, and I would like to walk you through a couple of scenarios from this release. We're delivering effective ways to enhance strategic flexibility, streamline complex processes, and build an agile enterprise for you.

Let's dive into the highlights,

The Strategic Foundation: Mastering Your Data in a Two-Tier ERP World 

Many organizations operate in a Two-Tier ERP landscape, with headquarters running on SAP Cloud ERP Private  (SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition). and agile subsidiaries operating on  SAP Cloud ERP, i.e., SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition.

In a product-centric two-tier ERP setup, master data objects such as customer and product master data become critical prerequisites for both tiers. This is where SAP Master Data Governance provides a strategic foundation.

 

By running SAP MDG at headquarters, organizations establish a single source of truth where all master data is created, validated, and approved before being distributed to target systems.

This approach follows a simple but crucial principle:

Create and govern master data centrally. Consume it locally.

Subsidiaries running on SAP Cloud ERP receive governed, high-quality master data, ensuring clean, compliant operations. This not only strengthens group-level reporting but also significantly accelerates the onboarding of new acquisitions. As your organization grows, your master data remains a stable and trusted asset.

For a deeper exploration of this topic, I recommend the blog post “SAP Master Data Governance in a Two-Tier ERPLandscape with HQ & Subsidiary Approach” by Abhijit Bolakhe.

 
Advanced Warehousing Meets Cloud Agility

Another flexible architectural approach we support is integrating a decentralized SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) system with SAP Cloud ERP. This hybrid model allows you to maintain a clean-core cloud ERP while leveraging the rich, mature, and highly configurable functionalities of a full-scale EWM for your complex warehouse operations.

 Let's look at a typical process. For inbound goods, a purchase order in SAP Cloud ERP creates an inbound delivery that is sent to your decentralized EWM. EWM then orchestrates the entire physical process from unloading to put-away. Once complete, confirmations flow back to the cloud ERP, perfectly updating stock and financials.

Similarly, for outbound, a sales order triggers the process, with EWM managing everything from picking to loading, and finally sending the goods issue confirmation back to SAP S/4HANA Cloud. This integration offers a flexible yet perfectly balanced approach, combining advanced warehouse power with cloud simplicity.
The 2602 release for SAP Cloud ERP continues our commitment to providing a flexible, intelligent ERP. For wider types of industry, these updates deliver the strategic control, process efficiency, and commercial agility needed to thrive.
Please make it a point to deep dive into these EWM integration scenarios. We have two blog posts by Sahil Grover  The SAP Extended Warehouse Management Integration with SAP Cloud ERP - Part 1 and The SAP Extended Warehouse Management Integration with SAP Cloud ERP - Part 2

I hope this overview has been insightful.

Thank you for reading.

entry.title: Company Code Carve out or Separation in SAP ERP system (SAP ECC & S4HANA On-Prem,Cloud environments)
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog outlines the typical business triggers that drive the need for Company Code Carve out in a productive ERP environment (ECC or S4HANA systems). It also highlights the possible realization methodologies to support such business transformations effectively.

Company Code Carve Out

A Company Code Carve-Out refers to the selective separation of data from a productive SAP instance into a separate target system. This data separation is driven by the organizational unit “Company Code” (technical domain: BUKRS) within the SAP system.

As part of the process, both higher-level organizational elements (e.g., Controlling Area, Operating Concern etc.) and lower-level hierarchical elements (e.g., Plant, Sales Organization, etc.) within the SAP enterprise structure are identified and analyzed to enable the separation of the entire legal entity’s business application data.

The dataset in scope typically includes all relevant data stored in SAP database tables and fields across all layers Viz. Customizing, Master, and Transactional data. This also encompasses customer-specific developments stored in Z or Y namespace tables.

Goal of Company Code Carve out is to give the divested business entity into an independent environment while minimizing the impact on the source system that contains the remaining data related to the relevant company codes.

Typical business use cases that require Company Code Carve out 

  1. Divesture of a Business Unit: It happens when the organization or group decides to sell off/dispose or close a part of the organization or subsidiary in order to raise cash/address regulatory or performance issues. With this activity, divested group can either become a stand-alone entity or independent company or strategic asset for a buyer.

  2. Country or Region-specific Separations/Decentralization of Central System: When an organization decides to spin-off or separates the country or region from their existing ECC or S4HANA landscape. These situations are primarily driven by the differences in the corporate laws, securities, regulations, tax rules and controls related to that country jurisdiction. Sometimes, this may also be driven due to the sanctions amended on specific countries leading to damage to the business in whole.

  3. Joint Venture: When organizations or partners decide to enter into JV with specific scope & duration to access country specific local markets, shared investments, risk diversifications and later decides to divest or spin off into a separate legal entity.

  4. Sale of Subsidiaries: An event occurs when parent organization plans to sale/transfer controls of a legal entity to another organization based on the board approvals, share holders’ resolution etc., for additional liquidity or strategic alignment on existing business.

  5. Strategic Refocus and Legal Restructuring: This kind of activity defines the business and reshapes the legal entity structure to support the strategy that organization thinks to refocusing their business. It helps in reallocation of capital to high advantage business or withdraw from misaligned businesses or non-core portfolio.

  6. Merger & Acquisitions: Mergers & Acquisitions are the transactions where organizations either combine or takes control over other to achieve strategic objectives/growth perspective. It provides opportunities to accelerate growth, portfolio diversification or consolidation.

All the aforementioned use cases require the complete historical transactional data of the respective legal entity to ensure uninterrupted business operations and to meet statutory and compliance reporting obligations.

Seamless operation and continuity of connected business applications are critical success factors for any divested or separated entity. Ensuring the continuity of open processes is essential for maintaining reporting dependencies and enabling informed strategic decision-making.

Furthermore, statutory reporting and audit trails of business application data represent critical data assets for any organization. They are fundamental to meeting country-specific data residency requirements and safeguarding the organization against potential legal and compliance risks arising from non-adherence to regulatory obligations.

To perform the separation of business entities/units (Company Code) from an existing SAP ERP landscape (ECC or S4HANA), SAP-DMLT service supports with SAP Company Code Carve Out solution that is tailored to address the aforementioned use cases with a cleaner strategy and data set that can let the organizations function independently and enables stable operations, financial reporting, auditing with the legal obligations.

Typically, objects in scope would be Company Codes and its relevant upper and lower hierarchical organizational elements (such as Controlling Area, Plant, Sales Org, associated master data – Customers, Vendors & Materials etc.)

Technical approach of Company Code Carve out

1. Company Code Clone & Delete

In this approach, the entire source system should be copied technically (clone) including repository objects, customizations, users and the business application data. Later, based on the user provided/confirmed company codes will be retained and all other company codes along with relevant org. units will be deleted from the cloned environment using SAP DMLT toolset.

This will technically delete the out-of-scope Company Codes along with its associated & shared data from target/cloned system, enables faster, cleaner and minimum disruption to business.

Business Outcomes (Value)

  1. Faster, Cleaner approach for Company Code Carve-out
  2. Reduced risk due to functional completeness
  3. Standard SAP tool to perform the carve out activity
  4. Limited testing required compared to general green field approach of only open balances
  5. Business Process Continuity & Faster realization
  6. Data history for statutory reporting needs

Transformation Scenario Limitations

  1. Suitable for smaller database
  2. Any additional data transformation expectation can't be embedded together

2. Company Code Transfer/Migration

As the name suggests, in this approach we will transfer / migrate only the relevant Company Codes with associated org. units along with the respective data sets only to the target empty system. It avoids the need for full copy of the source system compared to earlier explained 'Clone & Delete' approach.

P.S: Shell creation is the process of building a new & empty SAP system with complete repository, cross client objects and client specific customizing of source system without master & transaction data

Business Outcomes (Value)

  1. Minimal data footprint
  2. Lower infrastructure costs/repurpose any existing landscape
  3. Better control on the data set & objects (selection of relevant application) to be moved to target system/environment
  4. Possible to accommodate qualified transformations in any data harmonization (such as Rename of org objects, master data)
  5. Best suited for scenarios with a limited number of company codes being divested or for any country-specific carve outs - where the data set to be separated represents only a small or minimal portion of the overall database volume

Transformation Scenario Limitations

  1. Would require a longer project duration compared to clone-and-delete approach, as each application must be handled individually and might require additional, specific transformation capabilities embedded in the transfer logic.
  2. Relatively higher effort investment on validations and reconciliations

Realization Methodology of Company Code Carve-Out

Once a company code is productive or live with transactional data, any changes become restrictive. Moving configuration changes without transforming the already posted documents can lead to data inconsistencies in the live system, potentially causing critical disruptions in open business processes.

To separate any Company Code(s) from the live system, both the configuration, master and it’s associated transactional data must be handled consistently. For deletion of existing posted documents or transforming open documents, the SAP DMLT Company Code Carve Out Service is required. For more information, refer to the Company Code Carve Out Solution Brief.

Such data transformations in a live production system demand a highly precise and controlled changeover, ensuring consistency at the database level and seamless continuity of SAP software operations. Therefore, every relevant business scenario should be carefully reviewed in the system, considering current configurations and customizations across all connected application modules.

SAP DMLT offers a technical feasibility study to outline system data dependencies and prerequisite conditions. This is followed by a detailed customer workshop with the obtained statistics from the system. The realization of the transformation requirement is delivered under a separate professional consulting contract and executed as a project.

The overall delivery process is broadly divided into two parts:

1. Pre-Qualification of Requirement

It is essential to pre-qualify the business requirement to ensure the right-fit transformation solution is offered. This starts with gathering preliminary details about the business requirement and application usage within the system through a technical questionnaire, available at Mergers and Acquisitions, Divestments, Restructuring.

Based on the received responses, SAP can propose a feasibility study to assess the system, evaluate overall complexity, and deliver a detailed proposal. This assessment typically takes around two weeks, including the deployment of analysis tools in the system.

An SAP Solution Architect/Expert will perform the system assessment and present the key insights during a customer workshop session. This is followed by the provision of commercial details through the designated local SAP Market Unit account representatives.

2. Project Delivery

Depending on the complexity of the engagement, the required number of mock execution cycles will be determined during the scoping phase. In general, data transformation projects of this nature involve a minimum of 2-3 mock cycles before the production data cutover. The purpose of a mock execution is to simulate the complete production data carve-out/deletion, validate the results, and estimate the overall downtime required for the production system.

Each mock execution cycle includes several phases, which can be broadly classified as follows:

2.1 Technical Preparation Phase of Add-On (Uptime):

These types of transformation capabilities are deployed into the customer system as a separate add-on, delivered as a transportable content. The mock execution system should be a recent copy of the production system, with identical or closely matching hardware configuration that of the production environment. Once the tool content is deployed, the technical preparation phase begins, during which SAP DMLT experts prepare the transformation tool. This phase typically takes 2–3 weeks and includes uptime activities and the generation of technical programs for the relevant table-fields.

2.2 Data Carve-Out Phase (Downtime):

After the uptime preparatory activities are completed, the agreed business downtime begins. During this period, Company Code Carve-Out is performed directly at the database table-field level. All relevant Company Codes are separated along with the relevant Org Units in the respective application areas (e.g., FI-GL, FI-AA, ML, CO etc).

2.3 Post-Processing Testing Phase (Uptime):

Once the data carve-out phase is completed, the transformed system is handed over to the customer’s IT/application team for a sanity check during downtime. Upon successful completion of the sanity check, the transformed system is reopened for normal business operations.

Engagement with relevant SAP team or SAP Experts

If your business requirement involves any transformation related to Company Code aspects in a live, productive SAP ECC or S/4HANA system, you can engage with the SAP Data Management and Landscape Transformation (DMLT) consulting team through following ways;

  1. Create a Support Incident
    Submit an incident via the SAP ONE Support Launchpad under the component: CA-LT-SRV (Landscape Transformation Services), Or
  2. Contact via Email
    Reach out directly to the DMLT Global Consulting Experts Email: sap_dmlt_gce@sap.com

The SAP DMLT team will guide you through the pre-assessment process, feasibility evaluation, and project-based service engagement tailored to your specific system and transformation needs.

Reference SAP OSS Notes:

481938 - System Landscape Optimization Services

1462004 - SAP LT: Transformation Solution 'Company Carve-out'

Disclaimer: This blog is intended only for knowledge sharing about Company Code Carve-out/Delete service on SAP ERP (ECC or S/4 HANA) system which might help customer in their landscape transformation requirements. It should not be considered as guideline for performing such transformation services on their own in any of the SAP landscape. Any data transformation efforts should be thoroughly assessed in consultation with qualified subject matter experts, supported by a comprehensive system study, and aligned with the appropriate approvals from your auditors and relevant stakeholders.

entry.title: Sales in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
This blog shares highlights on Sales in SAP Cloud ERP 2602.

  • Advanced intercompany sales (5D2): enablement of sales kits
  • Manage Sales Documents app: initial scope
  • Input assistance in the SAP Fiori app Manage Customer Returns – Version 2
  • Data determination in access for pricing
  • Propagating Custom Fields from Product Master Data to Sales Document Item
  • New Microsoft Excel Export Formats for Price Management
  • Flexible Proposal of Validity Dates for Sales Contracts
  • Key User Extensibility for Billing Document Scheduling
  • Automatic Creation of Sales Orders from Unstructured Data
  • Customer Returns for Third-Party Sales with Variant Configuration
  • No Refund Option for Customer Returns Involving Suppliers
  • Detection of Overlapping Condition Records in Price Management
  • Introduction of More Fields for the Customer Invoice – Send (B2B) SOAP API
  • Returns Order OData v4 API: Enhancements Such as Serial Number and Address
  • Configuration of Customer-Specific Sales Order Types Using Sales Order Templates in SAP Central Business Configuration
  • Ability to Read Pricing Elements with the Preliminary Billing Document OData V4 API
  • Additional Capabilities of the Sales Order OData V4 API
  • BAdI for Customer-Specific Checks in Preliminary Billing Documents
  • Additional Capability for the Manage Sales Contracts - Version 2 App
  • Sales Contract OData V4 API - Additional Capability

Watch my video to get a glimpse of the new features:

 

Advanced intercompany sales (5D2): enablement of sales kits

​Sales kit (Sales BOM) processing can now be enabled in advanced intercompany sales. This feature increase operational efficiency and reduce complexity by using predefined products that consist of several components. This feature reduces manual effort and increase automation by using Monitor Value Chain to automatically post the intercompany purchase document and the intercompany sales order for inventory-controlled goods, and to monitor further and orchestrate the process.
 
The following graphic illustrates the business process.
 
Graphic: Advanced intercompany sales (5D2): enablement of sales kits

This capability lets a selling organization offer a sales kit while an affiliated delivering company fulfills it. A kit contains both inventory-controlled items and at least one non-inventory-controlled item; intercompany billing and stock movements apply only to the inventory-controlled components. The process supports multiple kit types already available in sell-from-stock, ranging from main-item level processing to bracket-style kits where sub-items carry pricing, inventory control, and delivery. When the delivering plant’s company code differs from the selling company’s, the advanced intercompany flow is triggered: the customer-facing sales order holds the full kit, while the intercompany PO, SO, and invoices include only the stock-managed sub-items. The outbound delivery shows the full kit, with goods movements on inventory-relevant sub-items, and the Value Chain Monitoring app automatically creates and orchestrates intercompany documents with end-to-end document flow visibility.

This enables multinational sales of complete kits without adding operational complexity, ensuring costs and revenues are correctly posted between affiliates for the inventory-managed components. Predefined multi-component products reduce manual steps and errors, while automation via Value Chain Monitoring minimizes effort and shortens cycle times. Supporting multiple kit types provides flexibility to fit varied pricing and fulfillment patterns, helping standardize cross-company operations and improve transparency. Overall, it boosts revenue opportunities with kits, streamlines intercompany processing, and enhances financial accuracy across delivering and selling entities.

For more information, see SAP Help Sales Kits in Advanced Intercompany Sales

The following table gives you an overview of the most important differences between the available ways of processing for items:

Table: Overview of Differences in Processing Sales Kit Items in Advanced Intercompany Sales

This video explains the concept.

 

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

Manage Sales Documents app: initial scope

The Manage Sales Document app is the central entry point to work with sales documents, initially covering Sales Orders and Sales Orders Without Charge. It provides rich, connected search across header, item, and schedule line levels, with refinements by common attributes and the option to add more fields, including the ability to search by one-time customer name. Users get context-appropriate actions at each level, can create different order types, and are routed to the right creation experience: the Fiori “Manage Sales Orders – Version 2” for supported types or the Web GUI for types not yet in Fiori. A highly requested grid-style experience is supported through flexible list personalization and the ability to export results to Excel or PDF.

The app consolidates tasks into a single, modern experience that preserves familiar workflows for VA05 users while expanding capabilities. The connected, multi-level search accelerates finding and acting on orders, reducing navigation overhead and time-to-resolution. Personalization and export features improve reporting, analysis, and handoffs to downstream processes, while context-aware navigation avoids disruption when certain document types still require the Web GUI. Together, these improvements enhance productivity, support real-world scenarios like one-time customers, and ease adoption by balancing continuity with innovation.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

Input assistance in the SAP Fiori app Manage Customer Returns – Version 2

The Manage Customer Returns - Version 2 app now provides real-time, AI-driven recommendations during returns order creation. After entering key context—like sales area and sold-to at header or product at item—the system suggests values such as return reasons, logistical follow-up activities, receiving plant, and even refund codes based on learned patterns from historical returns. Recommendations are ranked, with probabilities above 17% highlighted, and users can accept or adjust them before releasing the order. Activation can be turnkey if prerequisites are met: the Data Attribute Recommendation collection is set up and sufficient data volumes exist (roughly 3,000 eligible items in production and 1,000 in quality/test). Teams can also opt for manual training, disable specific scenarios, or cancel them as needed.

You will benefit from faster, more accurate data entry that reduces user effort and training time—especially for new or infrequent users—and improves process consistency. By leveraging historical patterns, the system helps standardize choices like follow-up activities and refund codes, lowering errors and rework and speeding customer resolutions. Governed activation and scenario control strike a balance between ease of adoption and enterprise oversight, making the capability scalable across different sales areas and product portfolios. Ultimately, the AI assistance shortens cycle times in returns handling, enhances customer satisfaction, and supports cleaner operational data for analytics and continuous improvement.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

Data determination in access for pricing

This release introduces the ability to create custom data fields and leverage them for data determination within pricing access sequences. The system can now identify and determine data that isn’t directly available in the business document, storing it in a dedicated structure (KOMPAZD) for use throughout subsequent pricing procedure steps. Key users can configure this data determination through access sequences defined by condition tables containing data fields, and create condition records where field values are determined based on factors like the product—eliminating the need to enhance the underlying document structure itself.

Organizations can now achieve more flexible and sophisticated pricing scenarios without modifying core business documents, streamlining implementation and maintenance efforts. This approach significantly reduces the volume of condition records needed, as data can be dynamically determined rather than explicitly stored across multiple record combinations. The result is a leaner, more maintainable pricing configuration that adapts to complex requirements while minimizing system overhead and administrative complexity.

Screenshot: Data determination in access for pricing

For more information, see: Extensibility: Data Determination in Access

 

Propagating Custom Fields from Product Master Data to Sales Document Item

Key users can now enable custom fields to automatically flow from product master data to sales document items through the Custom Fields app. When this business scenario is activated, the system automatically copies custom field values from the product master whenever a user creates a sales document item based on that product. Users retain the flexibility to modify these custom field values directly within the sales document item if needed.

This enhancement significantly streamlines the extensibility of sales documents by eliminating the need to manually enter custom field data across multiple touchpoints. Organizations can reduce complexity in maintaining custom fields across product master data and sales documents while improving overall efficiency in document processing and data consistency.

Screenshot: Propagating Custom Fields from Product Master Data to Sales Document Item

New Microsoft Excel Export Formats for Price Management

The Manage Prices – Sales app now supports exporting prices using enhanced Microsoft Excel formats, including proper "number" and "value" formatting where applicable. This means exported price data appears in native Excel formats rather than as plain text, making the data immediately usable for calculations and analysis.

Users can now leverage Excel's native functionalities directly on exported files without needing to reformat data manually. This improvement enhances productivity for pricing specialists who regularly work with exported price lists and need to perform calculations, create reports, or integrate pricing data with other business processes.

Screenshot: New Microsoft Excel Export Formats for Price Management

Flexible Proposal of Validity Dates for Sales Contracts

Organizations can now define rules for dynamically proposing valid-to dates on sales contracts. When creating a new sales contract, the system evaluates document header fields and applies specific calendar and offset rules to automatically propose appropriate validity dates. This leverages the configurable parameter and formula (CPF) framework for maximum flexibility.

This capability enables companies to tailor validity date management to their specific business requirements, reducing manual intervention and minimizing customer complaints caused by incorrect or incomplete validity dates during release order processing. The automation ensures consistency and accuracy across contract management processes.

Screenshot: Flexible Proposal of Validity Dates for Sales Contracts

Key User Extensibility for Billing Document Scheduling

Key users can now add up to five custom fields to the Schedule Billing Creation and Schedule Creation of Preliminary Billing Documents apps. Business users can then use these custom fields as selection parameters when scheduling billing document creation jobs, enabling precise filtering of which documents should be processed.

This extensibility empowers business users to tailor job selection criteria to their unique processes without requiring IT involvement or waiting for product enhancements. The precise filtering capability improves automation efficiency and ensures only the intended documents are processed during billing runs, increasing control and accuracy in billing operations.

Screenshot: Key User Extensibility for Billing Document Scheduling

Automatic Creation of Sales Orders from Unstructured Data

Internal sales representatives can now create sales orders directly from purchase order files in PDF or image formats. After uploading a file, the system automatically extracts relevant information into a sales order request and proposes field values—such as determining the sold-to party from extracted data. The sales order request can then be converted into a final sales order, with multilanguage support and individual training capabilities powered by SAP Document AI.

This automation dramatically increases sales force efficiency by eliminating manual data entry from paper or digital documents. Organizations can reduce processing time and human error while handling customer orders, allowing sales teams to focus on higher-value activities rather than administrative data transcription tasks.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

Customer Returns for Third-Party Sales with Variant Configuration

Organizations using scope item 4R6 (Sales Processing using Third-Party with Variant Configuration) can now process returns of configurable materials from end customers. When handling these returns, users can select logistical follow-up activities to ship materials back to the third-party supplier, and the system automatically transfers the variant configuration from the returns order to the returns purchase order.

This enhancement completes the end-to-end process for handling returns in third-party variant configuration scenarios. Companies can now efficiently manage the entire lifecycle of configurable products sold through third parties, ensuring seamless coordination between customer-facing returns processes and supplier return logistics.

Screenshot: Customer Returns for Third-Party Sales with Variant Configuration

No Refund Option for Customer Returns Involving Suppliers

Customer returns can now be processed with a "No Refund" control option when the return involves shipping goods back to a supplier. This applies to logistical follow-up activities for shipping to supplier or direct shipment to supplier, supporting scenarios where non-refundable goods—such as free samples provided by third-party suppliers—are returned.

This capability streamlines return processing by providing a cleaner, simpler workflow for specific return scenarios. Organizations can handle non-refundable returns more efficiently and accurately, improving operational speed while enhancing the overall customer experience through transparent and appropriate refund handling.

Screenshot: No Refund Option for Customer Returns Involving Suppliers

Detection of Overlapping Condition Records in Price Management

The Manage Prices – Sales app now displays a warning message when users create a condition record that overlaps with existing ones. An overlap occurs when the validity period of a new condition record conflicts with other records sharing the same condition type and price-relevant attributes. Previously, the system silently adjusted existing records without notification.

This proactive alerting improves collaboration among pricing specialists by making them aware of potential conflicts before they occur. Teams can review overlapping records and their validity periods, make informed adjustments, and maintain cleaner, more accurate pricing data across the organization.

Screenshot: Detection of Overlapping Condition Records in Price Management

Introduction of More Fields for the Customer Invoice – Send (B2B) SOAP API

The Customer Invoice – Send (B2B) SOAP API has been enhanced with additional fields at both the billing document header and item levels. New fields added to the PaymentTerms node include CustomerPaymentTermsName and payment term descriptions (PaymentTerm1Description, PaymentTerm2Description, PaymentTerm3Description). At the item level, the API now supports dates from preceding documents such as CustomerPurchaseOrderDate, DeliveryDate, and SalesDocumentCreationDate, along with reference information including SalesDocument, SalesDocumentItem, ReferenceSDDocument, ReferenceSDDocumentCategory, ReferenceSDDocumentItem, and CountryOfOrigin.

This enhancement ensures that important information is automatically transmitted to end customers through the API, supporting critical activities such as tracking key performance indicators and complying with specific regulations. Organizations can now provide more comprehensive invoice data to their B2B customers without manual intervention, improving data completeness and facilitating smoother downstream processes for both parties.

 

Returns Order OData v4 API: Enhancements Such as Serial Number and Address

The Returns Order OData v4 API (Customer Return A2X) has been enhanced with several key capabilities to support more comprehensive returns processing. Users can now leverage various new fields in the header and item entities, including purchase order type for ship-to party. Additionally, the API supports managing serial numbers for relevant returns order items and managing address data for business partners within returns orders.

These enhancements increase returns order processing efficiency for business integration scenarios. Organizations can now exchange more complete and accurate returns information with external systems, reducing manual data handling and enabling smoother automated workflows between SAP and connected applications or partners.

 

Configuration of Customer-Specific Sales Order Types Using Sales Order Templates in SAP Central Business Configuration

Organizations can now leverage SAP’s preconfigured best practice sales order types as templates to create customer-specific sales order type variants. A dedicated user application within SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) enables users to create and manage these custom sales order types, while also providing the ability to adjust the assignment of business processes to ensure consistency when introducing new customer-specific configurations.

This capability delivers a streamlined and flexible sales order process by allowing organizations to configure tailor-made sales order types that match their unique requirements. Internal sales representatives can operate more efficiently by utilizing a targeted subset of preconfigured business process capabilities, while the organization reduces overall configuration and maintenance effort through easy creation and deployment of customer-specific variants with minimal customization.

 

Ability to Read Pricing Elements with the Preliminary Billing Document OData V4 API

The Preliminary Billing Document OData V4 API has been enhanced with the capability to read pricing elements. Users can now retrieve pricing element data at both the header and item levels, including raw condition types and subtotal lines associated with preliminary billing document headers and items.

This enhancement provides comprehensive visibility into pricing structures within preliminary billing documents. Organizations can access detailed pricing element data effortlessly, improving transparency and understanding of how prices are calculated. The ability to retrieve condition types and subtotal lines facilitates accurate analysis and reporting, enabling finance and sales teams to better validate pricing accuracy and support downstream billing processes.

 

Additional Capabilities of the Sales Order OData V4 API

The Sales Order OData V4 API has been enhanced with significant new capabilities for processing sales orders. Users can now leverage multilevel variant configuration with more flexible identification methods for subinstances, access partner address data including phone number fields, and retrieve additional pricing element and sales order item fields. The API supports creating sales orders with reference to other sales documents, including the ability to change product parameters during this process. Additional capabilities include automatic assignment of sales contract items when creating sales orders, processing partner address data with one-time customer support, and retriggering pricing at both sales order and sales order item levels.

These enhancements unlock new revenue streams and business opportunities through smooth integration with external webshops, quoting solutions, and analytical engines. Organizations can simplify development and streamline maintenance by eliminating the need for multiple APIs, such as legacy Sales OData V2 APIs. The improvements lower total cost of ownership through robust, automated integration for order taking and processing, while harmonizing the integration experience by leveraging the latest features of the ABAP RESTful application programming model. Implementation timelines are accelerated by fully utilizing advanced OData V4 protocol features, reducing effort when setting up new integration scenarios.

Sales Order OData V4 API - Multi Level Variant Configuration - Soft Identification

Identification Options for Sub-Instances in Multi Level Variant Configurations

You now have different options to identify sub-instances in multi level variant configurations during create requets:

You can use one property or a combination of properties from the entity Variant Configuration Instance as follow:

  • IdentifierBOMItem

  • BillOfMaterialItemNumber and Product

  • IdentifierBOMItem, BillOfMaterialItemNumber, and Product

Sales Order OData V4 API - Field Enhancements (Address, Pricing Element, Item Entities)

Address Fields
  • For API_SALESORDER, new partner address fields are added to the Header Partner and Item Partner entities.
  • For API_SALESORDERSIMULATION: address fields are added to the Header Partner and Item Partner entities.

Pricing Element Fields
Fields have been added to the SalesOrderPricingElement and SalesOrderItemPricingElement entities of API_SALESORDER and API_SALESORDERSIMULATION

Item Entities
Fields have been added Sales Order Item entity of API_SALESORDER and API_SALESORDERSIMULATION.
 
Purchase Confirmation Status Check
The following entities have been updated for API_SALESORDER and API_SALESORDERSIMULATION:
  • Sales Order Header
  • Sales Order Item

Entity Update
Properties have been added to the Sales Order Header entity of API_SALESORDERSIMULATION
  • Sales Order (A2X, OData V4)
  • Sales Order - Simulate (A2X, OData V4)

 

BAdI for Customer-Specific Checks in Preliminary Billing Documents

A new business add-in (BAdI) enables organizations to implement custom logic for performing checks and blocks on preliminary billing documents. Using the BAdI Check of Data in Preliminary Billing Documents (SD_BIL_PBD_ACTION_CHECK) through the Custom Logic app, users can leverage header and item fields along with custom fields to block the finalization of preliminary billing documents, prevent the creation of billing documents from preliminary billing documents, and block the "Auto Finalize" action—instead creating the preliminary billing document with an "In Progress" status. When a block occurs, the system provides users with an error message containing relevant information.

This capability allows organizations to establish country-specific and industry-specific rules to monitor and restrict processes related to preliminary billing documents. Companies can manage complex billing procedures more effectively, particularly in the professional services industry, ensuring compliance with specific regulations and internal policies while improving overall user satisfaction through controlled and transparent billing workflows.

 

Additional Capability for the Manage Sales Contracts - Version 2 App

The Manage Sales Contracts - Version 2 SAP Fiori app has been enhanced with several new maintenance capabilities for comprehensive sales contract management. Users can now maintain contract-related partner functions including sold-to party and ship-to party directly within the app. Additional supported features include authorized partner checking rule maintenance, price list type maintenance, and sales contract description maintenance. The app also introduces new search help functionality for product hierarchy and product hierarchy node, enabling users to more efficiently locate and assign hierarchical product information within sales contracts.

These enhancements increase productivity by enabling users to maintain the majority of sales contract information directly in the modern SAP Fiori app without requiring duplicate effort in legacy transactions. Organizations benefit from improved user friendliness through the modern UI capabilities, streamlining the contract management experience and reducing the need to switch between different interfaces to complete contract-related tasks.

 

Sales Contract OData V4 API - Additional Capability

The advanced version of the Sales Contract OData V4 API has been enhanced with additional capabilities to create, read, and delete sales contracts and their entities. New supported fields include sales description on the sales contract header and price list type on the sales contract header, expanding the scope of contract data that can be managed through the API.

These enhancements improve holistic contract management by enabling seamless integration of sales contracts with external systems. Organizations benefit from improved performance through the new features offered in the ABAP RESTful application programming model, such as choosing between synchronous or asynchronous request processing and opting for minimal responses containing only the key of created entities. The API supports direct filtering of subentities and leverages a future-proof, stable architecture consistent across objects. Implementation is streamlined as no additional effort is required to set up a communication arrangement—the Sales Contract OData V4 API is included directly in the existing communication arrangement, saving valuable setup time.

  • Price list type enablement in Sales Contract OData V4 API

    A new property PriceListType has been added to the SalesContract entity of the Sales Contract (A2X, OData V4) (API_SalesContract)API. You can use this property to identify a price list or other condition type (for example, a surcharge or discount).

  • Sales description enablement in Sales Contract OData V4 API

    A new property SalesContractDescription has been added to the SalesContract entity of the Sales Contract (A2X, OData V4) (API_SalesContract) API. You can use this property as a text description of a sales contract that can be used as a search term for locating the sales contract.

 

Feel free to follow us in the SAP community and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates.

Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, 2026, we hosted a series of compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 product release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.  You can find the recordings of your past sessions on SAP Learning. As a partner, you will find the recording and presentation for Manufacturing as well as other topics of interest via the Empowering SAP partners site.

🔍 Find all our assets  

The release 2602 showcases more 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you:

entry.title: What’s New in My Home SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 Release?
entry.content:
My Home already serves as a centralized entry point for business users by providing immediate access to key insights, supporting the efficient management of tasks and situations, and enabling users to organize their favorite pages and apps. This helps streamline daily operations and reduces the time spent navigating across the system. The 2602 release builds on this foundation by enhancing how information is delivered, personalized, and consumed—ensuring that users can focus on what matters most.  

The 2602 release of My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition introduces a range of new delivered and beta features, along with meaningful UX enhancements designed to elevate the overall user experience. In this blog, we’ll explore what’s new in this release and how these updates help business users work more efficiently and stay better informed. 

 

Mandatory News – Ensuring critical information is seen by all business users  

With the 2508 release, administrators gained the ability to deliver timely and targeted updates directly to business users through the My Home centralized news feed. The 2602 release takes this a step further by introducing mandatory news articles. News articles containing essential company information can now be marked as required reading. Business users must acknowledge these articles, confirming that they have read and understood the content. This feature plays a critical role in ensuring compliance with legal, safety, and policy-related requirements. It also helps eliminate knowledge gaps by ensuring that all employees receive the same information. During periods of change—such as the rollout of new policies or process updates—mandatory reading ensures that users understand both the reasons for the change and its potential impact, improving alignment across the organization. 

 An example of a news article that is required reading. Alt Text: A screenshot of My Home with a pop-over “Required Reading” that is also highlighted with “Contains Critical News”. The news article has an image at the top, followed by text showing the first part of the article. The “Done” button is greyed out, the “Remind me later” button is visible.

 

The Manage News app has a toggle to mark articles as “Required Reading”. Alt Text: Screenshot of the Manage News app with the toggles set to “Yes”, and some news text in the Description field.

 

News and Pages visibility - A more focused experience 

In the 2602 release we introduced enhanced customization options, allowing business users to control the visibility of news and pages within My Home. This enables users to tailor their workspace according to their role, priorities, and daily responsibilities. By showing only relevant information and tools, My Home becomes more focused and clutter-free, helping users stay productive and work more efficiently without unnecessary distractions. 

My Home with enhanced personalization, showing News deselected and Pages selected. Alt Text: A screenshot of My Home, with eight cards in one row in the Pages section, below the ToDos section showing one situation and the Apps section.

 

My Home Settings pop-over with the new selectors for News and Pages within the News and Pages section. Alt Text: A screenshot showing the Layout part of My Home Settings, where users can select or deselect sections. For the News and Pages section, News is deselected, Pages are selected.

 

Improved Mobile Experience with horizontal scrolling 

For business users on the move, the My Home mobile experience has been enhanced with horizontal scrolling. This update supports visual browsing and improves the discoverability of tasks, apps, and insight cards on smaller screens. As a result, mobile users can navigate content more intuitively and access important information quickly—wherever they are. 

 

AI-assisted Content Addition - Adding apps using natural language. 

My Home enables you to do your job with speed and agility. Even more so now that our 2508 Beta AI – Assisted content addition feature is now available in our 2602 release as a premium feature.  Business users can now add applications using natural language in two clicks. The business user can review and verify the results generated by AI ensuring accuracy.  This saves time by dramatically simplifying the process of adding new applications and tiles to My Home. Now with this new AI assisted feature,  there is no longer a need for business users to discover the appropriate application or even to navigate away from their home page to the application and manually apply filters to find the information they require — thanks to Generative AI, the relevant information can be surfaced quickly and effortlessly using natural language. This significantly reduces the effort needed to discover and add Insights Cards. 

AI-Assisted Smart Personalization of My Home for Applications. Alt Text: The image shows three screenshots: at the top an extract of My Home showing the blue salutation bar, with an AI button “Add Content” on the right. An arrow has been added linking it to the second screenshot, where a popover “Add Content” is shown, where the user has entered “I want to monitor sales orders what app should I use to do this”, and two of four results are listed and visualized: “Track Sales Orders” and “Manage Sales Orders” (shown as an insight tile). Below that a screenshot of the outcome in My Home: the former is added to Apps Favorites, the latter to Insights Tiles.

 

Smart Helpers - Automating Repetitive Tasks 

Business users now have access to the Beta  AI – assisted task automation in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 release. It allows key users and business users to automate their daily activities in SAP’s Fiori apps by using generative AI to create so call “smart helpers”. Those “smart helpers” execute the repetitive activities on behalf of the business user either from the SAP Fiori apps while always keeping the business user in the loop for decisions on data changes.  Business users simply describe their desired business outcome in natural language, related to a specific SAP Fiori elements-based application, and the smart helper will take care of it for you. 

Beta: The planned way of giving users an overview of the AI-assisted task automation smart helpers in My Home. Alt Text: Screenshot of My Home with a section “Smart Helpers” above the “Apps” section, with two tabs “Smart Helpers (4)” and “Active Runs (3)”. The latter is selected, showing three cards with title, description and executed step shown: one is completed, two are running.

 

Beta: Manage Smart Helpers application for defining instructions for the planned AI-assisted task automation. Alt Text: Screenshot showing the instruction text at the top, an empty section for attachments, and the execution plan with four steps: read order requests, analyze request data, simulate pending requests, create sales orders.

 

AI-assisted Smart Solution for situations – Simplifying workflow 

The Beta AI-assisted Smart Solution for Situations in My Home simplifies workflows by dynamically adapting the user interface to guide business users through resolution steps using the power of generative AI. Key information and actions are surfaced though dynamic cards reducing the need for in-depth navigation.  One click resolution of situations directly from the homepage thereby enhancing efficiency. There is support for bank messages for alerts, reaching project end dates when a project stage is not completed and sales quotation expiry situations. 

Beta: AI-Assisted Smart Solution for Situations in My Home. Alt Text: An image with two screenshots: the upper one show the top part of My Home showing five Situation cards in the To-Dos section. One titled “End Date for Project Digital Media Assessment for Media Corp…” is linked with an arrow to an expanded card shown in the second screenshot – the card is taller and wider, contains a summary on the left with two action buttons “Assign To Me” and “Close Situation”, and more details on the right.

 

Final Thoughts 

The My Home 2602 release demonstrates our continued focus on usability, personalization, and effective communication. From mandatory news articles that drive accountability to enhanced customization and mobile usability, these improvements help ensure that My Home remains a powerful and user-centric entry point to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 Beta AI-assisted features such as Beta AI-assisted Smart solution for situations and Smart Helpers are now available for Beta Testing with the 2602 release. At SAP, we’re committed to delivering the best experience possible, which is why we’re inviting you to join our Beta Testing Program. By participating, you’ll get early access to these features, have the chance to share your feedback, and help us fine-tune them before the official launch. Your input will play a key role in shaping the quality and usability of these new capabilities.  

Also, If you are interested in hearing about the latest innovations in SAP Cloud ERP, please join our Public Cloud Practitioner Forum in which SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers share best practices and provide feedback to the SAP development teams about new functionality.

entry.title: Inbound, Outbound, Certificate Bound: Certificate Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Certificates are key to secure integrations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but their roles often cause confusion. This post clarifies the different certificate types and shows how to manage them using three key SAP Fiori apps.

Introduction

Navigating certificate terms like TLS, client authentication, and certificate mapping can be a challenge at first. Fortunately, understanding their usage in S/4HANA Cloud makes it easier to secure connections and authenticate users or systems. We'll cover:

  • Certificates in S/4HANA Cloud: their purpose and types
  • How S/4HANA Cloud acts as both client and server
  • Which SAP Fiori apps to use to manage certificates

Certificate Basics

For a detailed explanation of what certificates are and how they work, see this blog post: Beyond Basic (1): Certificate-Based Authentication. Here, we just recap the main usage types.

TLS Server Certificates: Know Who You Called

TLS (Transport Layer Security) is a widely used internet standard for encrypting and validating connections between systems. It’s not unique to S/4HANA Cloud—almost all web communications rely on TLS for encryption and to confirm that the client is connecting to the intended server.
When a client initiates a connection, it checks the remote server’s TLS certificate against a list of trusted certificates (more specifically: it checks the certificate and compares the issuer of the certificate against a list of trusted issuers).

Client Certificates: Know Who Called You

Once the connection is secured by TLS and the client has verified the server’s identity, the next step is for the server to confirm that the client is authorized to connect. This authentication process works similarly to traditional user/password logins, but instead of a password, the client presents a certificate. This certificate-based method is a more secure way for the server to verify the client’s identity.

Client and Server Roles in Communication

In any communication, there’s a client (the caller) and a server (the responder). The client always checks the server’s identity via a TLS certificate, and the server can verify the client’s identity through a client certificate.

S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can act as either client or server, depending on whether it’s making outbound connections or accepting inbound ones. Depending on the role the S/4 system takes, it either needs to present a client certificate to authenticate itself to the remote server (when acting as a client), or verify the client certificate of the incoming call (when acting as a server).

Three Maintenance Apps for Certificates

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, there are three key Fiori apps to manage certificates:

  • Maintain Certificate Trust List: List and manage trusted certificate authorities (CAs) for outbound server validation
  • Maintain Client Certificates: Store and export certificates for outbound client authentication
  • Maintain Communication Users: Map inbound certificates to communication users for secure access

The maintained certificates are then referenced in the Communication Systems app and the Communication Arrangements app.

Outbound Server Validation (TLS server certificates)

When S/4HANA Cloud connects outbound, it validates the remote server’s certificate against the Certificate Trust List. The Manage Certificate Trust List app lets you add and manage trusted Certificate Authorities (CAs)—the issuing authorities of the server’s certificate.
Good to know: every SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenant comes with an SAP-managed list of trusted CAs (similar to the pre-delivered trust list in your browser). So most of the time, you don’t have to do much here.

As explained above, this TLS server validation is not related to authenticating specific users; it’s simply a prerequisite for establishing a secure connection.

Outbound Client Authentication

When S/4HANA Cloud connects to another system as a client, it may present a certificate for authentication. These certificates are stored in the Maintain Client Certificates app.
Good to know: every SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenant comes with an SAP-managed client certificate preinstalled. But you can of course create your own.

The specific certificate to be used for a connection is then referenced in the Communication Systems app under “Users for Outbound Communication” and is then selected in the communication arrangement for the specific outbound integration.

Inbound Certificate Mapping

External systems connecting to S/4HANA Cloud present their certificates, which are mapped to communication users. The user mapping happens in the Maintain Communication Users app. There you upload the client certificate (just the public key!) of the caller. This maps the communication user to the subject and issuer attributes of the client certificate.

Important to know:

  • the client's certificate is not “pinned”, so rotating the certificate is possible without re-upload (unless the issuer or subject change, of course).
  • only client certificates signed by specific CAs are accepted by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The list of these CAs cannot be changed but is predetermined by SAP and available in SAP Note 2801396.  In particular: this list is not the one maintained in the Manage Certificate Trust List app.

Conclusion

Grasping the various certificate types and their roles is crucial, though it can be complex. The main factor in successfully handling certificates is understanding your communication scenario—whether your system operates as a client or a server. This knowledge guides you in choosing the appropriate app for configuring the correct settings, ensuring your communication environment remains secure.

entry.title: GRC, Trade and Tax with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm excited to share the update regarding our latest release - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 for GRC, Trade, and Tax. This update highlights many significant innovations from SAP Risk and Assurance Management, SAP Watch List Screening, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, as well as SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance.

For a quick overview of the top release highlights for GRC with 2602, you can watch the video below.

Video 1. Release Highlight Video for GRC in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602

This blog covers the following innovations:

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

  • AI-assisted trend analysis and summary for reports (beta)
  • Embedded analytics to facilitate VAT adjustments
  • E- verify and Automated Handling of Draft VAT Returns from Authorities
  • Enabling Electronic Invoices Through KSeF 2.0 for Poland​

SAP Risk and Assurance Management

  • Extended analytical and reporting capabilities: new reporting dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud, enterprise edition

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection

  • Intelligent alert handling and investigation recommendations
  • Management-focused monitoring dashboard

SAP International Trade

  • Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

​SAP Watchlist Screening

  • Delta Screening​

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

AI-assisted trend analysis and report summary

Tax accountants often spend significant time validating data by comparing current tax figures with previous returns. To accelerate this process, we introduce AI-assisted trend analysis for VAT returns. This feature helps tax experts by automatically uncovering hidden trends, flagging outliers, and detecting root causes for discrepancies. It provides intelligent summaries that can be used to simplify the approval process and support future audits. This innovation allows the team to move from reactive validation to proactive analysis.

Picture 1. AI-assisted trend analysis and report summary

Value Proposition​
  • Reduce the time needed to interpret VAT data across periods, leading to faster reviews, quicker approvals, and shorter reporting cycles.
  • Accuracy is improved and compliance risk is reduced. This helps identify anomalies early, minimizing manual errors and lowering the risk of incorrect filings.
  • Easy to make clear and informative data-driven decisions.
Capabilities
  • The analysis highlights key trends, fluctuations, and irregularities, and flags values that help to identify potential errors or compliance risks. ​
  • Provide concise summary of key VAT insights to support faster reviews, approvals, and decision-making.​

Embedded analytics to facilitate VAT adjustments

During the preparation of statutory reports, tax experts require deep visibility into their data to ensure accuracy. With the 2602 release, the 'Manage Tax Items' activity within SAP Document and Reporting Compliance is enhanced with a powerful ''analyze data" option.

This provides tax experts with additional fields and the flexibility to analyze tax data to identify anomalies. Now you can easily see how invoices are mapped to your VAT returns and pinpoint any documents that might have been excluded, enabling seamless adjustments integrated directly with accounting. To further tailor the solution, you can now use extensibility to plug in your own custom analytics CDS views, allowing you to choose between standard SAP-delivered queries or your own defined views for analysis.

 

Video 2. Embedded analytics to facilitate VAT adjustments

Value Proposition​
  • Advances the VAT review and adjustment by integrating analytics.
  • Improves accuracy and strengthens compliance through data-driven decisions.
  • Reduces manual effort and reliance on offline analysis and external tools​.
  • Enhances analytical transparency and enables faster and more confident decision-making.​
Capabilities ​
  • Embedded analytics within the Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting application​
  • Direct access to detailed VAT tax line-item records with added data points​
  • Interactive analytical views with charts, filters, and drill-down capabilities​
  • Trend analysis and comparisons across multiple VAT dimensions​
  • Identification of documents for inclusion in or exclusion from VAT returns​
  • Analysis at the point of action without switching applications​
  • Support for efficient VAT reconciliation and compliance preparation​

For more information, see: Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting | SAP Help Portal

E- verify and Automated Handling of Draft VAT Returns from Authorities

A growing number of tax authorities are providing pre-populated, or draft, tax returns based on real-time transactional data. Our latest update helps you automate the handling of these draft returns, ensuring consistency with your company's books. With this feature, you can pull auto-populated returns from an authority's portal and automatically compare them with the returns prepared in your ERP system to identify any mismatches.

Based on the reconciliation results, you can then process actions such as submitting a delta file, accepting, or rejecting the draft. This streamlines the entire process, from review and correction to the submission of the final return.

 

Video 3. E- verify and Automated Handling of Draft VAT Returns from Authorities

Enabling electronic invoices through KSeF 2.0 for Poland

With the Polish Ministry of Finance enforcing the use of electronic invoices through the KSeF 2.0 platform beginning in February 2026, we are equipping you to meet this mandate head-on. SAP Document and Reporting Compliance now enables you to generate B2B e-invoices in the required XML format from both Finance (FI) and Sales & Distribution (SD), including tax invoices, correction invoices, down-payment invoices, and self-billing invoices. You can submit these invoices interactively to KSeF 2.0, visualize them in a human-readable PDF format with a QR code, and retrieve supplier invoices for automated posting.

Picture 2. Enabling electronic invoices through KSeF 2.0 for Poland

SAP Risk and Assurance Management

Extended analytical and reporting capabilities: new reporting dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud, enterprise edition

We are introducing the new Risk and Compliance Cockpit, an SAP Analytics Cloud story that gives you an instant, comprehensive overview of your organization's compliance landscape.

This dashboard empowers compliance managers to identify process inefficiencies by visualizing compliance issues organized by type and status, with charts showing trends over time. It makes it easy to compare current performance against historical data, highlight long-term trends, and identify deviations. With insights into average issue resolution times and year-to-year analysis, this single source of truth helps you prioritize critical unresolved issues and improve operational efficiency.

 

Video 4.  Extended analytical and reporting capabilities: new reporting dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud, enterprise edition

Value Proposition​
  • Get an instant overview of compliance issues, organized by type and status​
  • Improved operational efficiency supported by trend and year-to-year analysis​
  • Charts visualize trends over time​
  • Overview of what to prioritize by highlighting critical unresolved issues​
​Capabilities ​
  • Identify inefficiencies in organizations' processes​
  • Instant overview of compliance issues​
  • Filter business data to examine specific categories of compliance issues​
  • Visual and comparative analysis of data​
  • Compare current performance while highlighting long-term trends and deviations

For more information, see: Risk and Compliance Cockpit

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection

Intelligent alert handling and investigation recommendations

In SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition, investigations are the central hub for examining security incidents. This new capability automates and enhances this critical process.

The primary value of this feature is to decrease the manual effort required by SAP monitoring agents and increase their overall efficiency. When an agent prepares an investigation to send to a customer, they are now supported with automatic, case-specific response and mitigation recommendations.

This helps teams by automatically compiling investigation results and formulating suggestions for customers on how to address the alerts. The system intelligently generates these recommendations based on the standard operating procedure of the specific pattern that created the alert, further enhanced by predefined value lists and machine learning functionality. This ensures that the advice provided is both highly relevant and immediately actionable, allowing your security teams to move from detection to resolution faster and more effectively.

Picture 3. Intelligent alert handling and investigation recommendations

For more information, see: Generating Investigation Recommendations

Management-focused monitoring dashboard

We are introducing a powerful new monitoring dashboard within SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition, specifically designed for management roles. In today's complex security landscape, having a clear, high-level view of your organization's cyberhealth is not just an advantage; it is a necessity. This new dashboard is for all managers and security leaders who need to quickly understand current security events, alerts, and ongoing investigations to make informed, timely decisions.

It facilitates a deeper understanding of complex security data by offering a comprehensive picture of your system's cyberhealth and protection status. The dashboard also shows the distribution of security events by various criteria, effectively highlighting the most affected systems, patterns, and users to help you focus resources where they are needed most.

To ensure the information is tailored to your specific needs, you can also create your own personalized monitoring pages with a selection of charts that are most relevant to your responsibilities..

Picture 4. Management-focused monitoring dashboard

For more information, see: Using the Monitoring Dashboard

SAP International Trade

Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

For businesses operating with plants abroad, ensuring the correct export declaration is crucial. In a plant abroad scenario, this feature ensures that the country of the delivering plant, rather than the country of the company code, determines the correct foreign trade organization for the customs declaration. This enhancement improves compliance with international trade regulations for businesses leveraging the "Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad" (RITA) scope.

Value Proposition
  • Enhance compliance with trade regulations by ensuring the correct foreign trade organization is used for export declarations.
Capabilities 
  • Manage your export declaration in SAP Global Trade Services using the foreign trade organization within the country of the plant abroad.
  • Identify plants abroad on pro forma invoice line items by considering countries where registration for indirect taxation (RITA) is enabled.

In SAP Cloud ERP,  a billing document exists where goods delivery is performed via a foreign plant, for example, the plant country is different from the country of the company code.

Picture 5. Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

In SAP GTS, Edition for SAP HANA, the FTO of the corresponding export declaration is now determined based on the legal unit corresponding to the foreign plant used in the billing document.

Picture 6. Export declaration for plants abroad in the context of Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA)

For more information, see: Enablement of Plants-Abroad for Export Processing and Foreign Plant​

Delta screening with SAP Watch List Screening and manual release of blocked document items

Continuous compliance with sanction party lists is critical. Screening requests sent to SAP Watch List Screening will now remain active for delta screening for up to one month. This means the system automatically performs delta screenings against any changes to watch lists and updates the status of your business partners and documents accordingly, providing automatic coverage without requiring new manual screenings.

Additionally, for situations requiring manual intervention, the "Manage Documents - Trade Compliance" Fiori app now allows authorized users to manually release a trade compliance document item that is blocked. This creates a simple, straightforward, and auditable overrule process to comply with legal requirements while ensuring business continuity.

Picture 7. Delta screening with SAP Watch List Screening and manual release of blocked document items

For more information, see: Integration with SAP Watch List Screening and Delta Screening in SAP Watch List Screening

Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Artificial Intelligence as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below: 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on Hiruni Arunodhi Badulle | LinkedIn.

entry.title: Core Logistics in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to the 2602 release of SAP Cloud ERP where we have introduced a set of enhancements focused on strengthening day-to-day supply chain execution and planning. These updates help streamline key logistics activities and improve user experience, so teams can execute faster, improve material visibility, and make more reliable, data-driven decisions across the end-to-end logistics flow.  In this blog post, you'll find an overview of the key new innovations and highlight features being rolled out with this release.

Short on time? The below video gives a summary of the top 3 highlights from this release.


 

 



Inventory Management

In the inventory management area, new features and apps have bene introduced to improve efficiency and accuracy when managing and moving physical inventory.

Streamlining Physical Inventory Management with new app "Manage Physical Inventory Item List" 

Managing physical inventory is about far more than counting stock: it’s about ensuring every item is tracked, verified, and processed correctly across potentially hundreds or thousands of line items, multiple locations, and multiple documents. In this kind of environment, speed and accuracy are critical. With this in mind, in the 2602 release of SAP Cloud ERP, SAP introduces the new Fiori app Manage Physical Inventory Item List (F8634)—giving inventory managers one clear, powerful place to manage physical inventory items across all relevant documents. The new app is designed to bring clarity and structure to this time-sensitive process by offering intuitive, user-friendly list reports and comprehensive status visibility so users can immediately see what needs attention and what’s already complete.

When you open the app, you start with flexible filtering options that let you tailor the list to the exact scenario you’re working on—such as narrowing down by plant and storage location to generate an overview for a specific area. Filters can be expanded easily, making it simple to adapt the screen to different use cases. From the resulting list, you can select items for further processing and execute key follow-up actions directly in the same place—including posting, triggering a recount, or printing—without jumping between applications. This is where the app really shines: it enables managers to filter physical inventory items by processing status (for example, showing only Counted items) so they can focus on the next required step and move work forward efficiently across multiple inventory documents.

A typical end-to-end scenario highlights the productivity gains. After physical inventory documents have been created and warehouse staff have completed counting, the inventory manager can filter by planned count date and then refine the list by status—such as showing only counted items.
