From there, it’s easy to review count details, identify quantity differences, and decide the right follow-up action. Items with no difference can be mass-selected and posted in just a click. Items with large discrepancies can be selected and sent for recount, where the user defines a recount date and (if needed) adds header information—automatically generating a new physical inventory document so warehouse teams know exactly what to recount. For marginal differences, the manager can still proceed with posting based on business rules, and if the system is configured with reason codes, the app prompts users to select the appropriate code during posting—supporting compliant, auditable processing aligned with company requirements. Finally, managers can confirm outcomes by filtering for posted items and, when needed, navigate directly from the list to the created material documents for review and further processing.

Overall, Manage Physical Inventory Item List (F8634) increases inventory transparency and helps teams complete inventory processing faster and with fewer errors. By combining comprehensive list reporting, efficient cross-document processing, and status-driven filtering in a single, streamlined experience, the app supports inventory managers with the clarity they need to keep physical inventory moving from count to resolution—accurately, quickly, and with full control.

 

 

Supporting Intra-Plant Stock Transfer Reservations

In many plants, materials have to be moved between storage locations to keep production and operations running—often at short notice and under time pressure. While these movements are typically planned using reservations (created manually or generated by MRP), execution has historically required warehouse staff to switch between apps or rely on classic processing such as MIGO—adding effort and increasing the risk of mistakes. With this new enhancement, intra-plant stock transfers become significantly easier and faster: warehouse teams can now use the Manage Reservation Items app to post stock transfers directly from a reservation for both manual and MRP-generated reservations using movement type 311, all in one place.

In practice, users filter and review the relevant reservation items, select multiple lines at once, and create the goods movement as a single, streamlined action—completing any missing details as needed before posting. Once posted, the system automatically creates two material document entries—one for the goods issue and one for the goods receipt—so the transfer is fully captured and traceable without extra steps. The result is clear business value: mass posting of several reservation items in one go, improved usability through integrated goods movement posting, and more time-efficient processing for busy warehouse teams.

 

Monitor Material Documents More Effectively : Material Documents Overview Enhancements 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP’s Material Documents Overview app (F1077) is a modern SAP Fiori replacement for the classic MB51 experience—built to help you review and analyze goods movements with a smoother, more intuitive UX while closing remaining functional gaps step by step. In one place, users can search, filter, sort, and drill into material documents to understand what moved, when it posted, and how it impacts operations and stock. This transition is more than “nice to have”: moving to the new app is essential because legacy options will ultimately be deprecated, and SAP is actively enhancing F1077 based on real customer feedback to make switching worthwhile.

Driven directly by customer input, SAP has been evolving the app’s functional scope and usability to better match how supply chain and warehouse teams actually work. Recent enhancements include the availability of additional fields—notably amount in local currency and sales value—to make financial and commercial context visible alongside logistics data. The app also now supports signed quantity, reducing the mental gymnastics of interpreting directionality across receipts and issues. And for users who regularly pivot between quantity-focused and value-focused analysis, SAP introduced a variant that displays amounts and values, making it easier to switch perspectives and tailor the overview to different roles and reporting needs.

In the 2602 release, the app takes a more visual and analytical leap forward with two planned improvements that make high-volume review work noticeably smoother. First, color coding for selected quantities (such as Signed Quantity)helps users identify the right figures at a glance, reducing errors and speeding up interpretation during daily checks. Second, sums for quantities and values add instant rollups for quick validation and comparison—particularly useful when reconciling multiple postings or checking totals across filtered selections. Together, these 2602 features continue the push to improve usability while enhancing the functional scope in the goods movement process, keeping the Material Documents Overview app firmly positioned as a practical tool for operational control in SAP Cloud ERP.

 


Handling Unit Management

Effective Handling Unit Management is fundamental to an integrated and transparent supply chain. Our latest updates to SAP Cloud ERP introduce significant enhancements in this area, designed to increase operational flexibility, improve system integration, and ensure alignment with global standards

Terminology Alignment with Global Standards for SSCC

To promote clarity and ensure consistency with global industry practices, we have updated the terminology used in the configuration activities for Serial Shipping Container Code (SSCC) numbering. The term "ILN basis number" has been replaced with "GS1 company prefix." This alignment with current GS1 standards helps reduce ambiguity during system setup and ensures your organizations handling unit identification is compliant with international norms. This change enhances usability for global enterprises without altering any underlying system logic.

Interface Support for Empty Handling Unit Creation

To provide greater flexibility for integration scenarios, the OData API for Handling Unit Management now supports the creation of empty handling units. This enhancement enables external systems or custom applications to first create a handling unit container and then subsequently manage the packing of materials into it. This capability allows organizations to model real-world logistics processes more accurately, where containers are often prepared before their contents are finalized. This facilitates more sophisticated and responsive integration with warehouse automation systems and third-party logistics platforms.

 

New Business Event for Handling Unit Creation

We are introducing a new business event that is triggered upon the creation of a handling unit within an Inventory Management-managed storage location. This event-driven mechanism enables real-time communication with external systems, such as parcel service providers or transportation management platforms, instantly notifying them when a handling unit is ready. This capability allows organizations to automate downstream processes, improve the timeliness of data exchange, and enhance end-to-end visibility, thereby reducing manual coordination and improving overall supply chain responsiveness.

 

Integration with External Output Management Systems

This release introduces a new feature that allows for the integration of Handling Unit Management with external, best-of-breed output management systems. By enabling the external channel in your output parameter determination, you can now send raw data for handling unit labels and documents directly to a preferred third-party solution. This provides organizations with maximum flexibility to design complex form layouts, manage specialized printing hardware, and utilize advanced output channels. The external system processes the output and sends a status confirmation back to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ensuring full process traceability and control.

 

 

Further Services to Support Partial Goods Receipt for Inbound Delivery with Third-Party Logistics Warehouse Management (Release 2508.2) 

We are excited to introduce a significant enhancement to our warehouse logistics capabilities: the ability to post partial goods receipts for inbound deliveries from your third-party Warehouse Management System (WMS). This feature directly addresses a common bottleneck at the receiving dock by empowering your warehouse staff to begin the putaway process immediately after unloading parts of a delivery, rather than waiting to check the entire shipment. This new flexibility accommodates a wide range of scenarios, allowing you to process complete or fractional quantities for single or multiple delivery items, both with and without handling units. This ultimately accelerates inventory availability and dramatically improves warehouse throughput.

To ensure seamless data integrity, this process is supported by robust, automated communication between SAP S/4HANA Cloud and your WMS. For every partial receipt posted, a confirmation is sent to the WMS, and a straightforward process allows for the cancellation of any erroneous postings. Once all parts of the delivery have been received, the inbound delivery in SAP S/4HANA Cloud is automatically updated to reflect the final, accurate totals for quantity, weight, and volume. A final confirmation is then sent to the WMS, guaranteeing that your core ERP system is perfectly synchronized with the physical reality documented by your warehouse partner.
